_comment: Lafayette Park — authored 4-corner polygon. Single source of truth for the park's face geometry (curb, asphalt mouths, ribbons, fence). Replaces the OSM leisure=park 41-vertex trace as the geometry authority — see cartograph/FEATURES.md 'The ribbon doctrine' for why polygons (not chains) are load-bearing for corner geometry. Corners derive from PARK_HALF=175m half-width on the park's natural axes (axis-aligned ±a), then rotate by tiltDegrees into the compass frame. Order: SW, SE, NE, NW (CCW in compass frame).
name: Lafayette Park
